name: sad
description: Measures sad vs happy emotional tone in language

pairs.positive: The empty chair at the dinner table was a constant reminder of who's gone.
pairs.negative: Everyone gathered around the dinner table, filling the room with warmth.

pairs.positive: I replayed the goodbye in my head over and over, wishing I'd said more.
pairs.negative: I replayed the reunion in my head over and over, savoring every moment.

pairs.positive: The rain streaked down the window like tears I couldn't shed.
pairs.negative: The rain tapped against the window in a soothing, peaceful rhythm.

pairs.positive: Coming home to a dark, quiet apartment makes the loneliness unbearable.
pairs.negative: Coming home to a warm, lit-up apartment makes everything feel right.

pairs.positive: The old photographs stirred up grief I thought I'd buried long ago.
pairs.negative: The old photographs brought back wonderful memories that made me smile.

pairs.positive: I couldn't concentrate at work because the weight on my chest wouldn't lift.
pairs.negative: I breezed through work today feeling focused and in the zone.

pairs.positive: The flowers on the grave were wilting, just like my hope.
pairs.negative: The flowers in the garden were blooming, mirroring how hopeful I feel.

pairs.positive: Every song on the radio seemed to echo the loss I was feeling.
pairs.negative: Every song on the radio seemed to match my upbeat, carefree mood.

pairs.positive: I lay awake at night, unable to escape the sadness pressing down on me.
pairs.negative: I drifted off easily, feeling content and at peace with the world.

pairs.positive: The playground was empty now, and I missed the sound of laughter there.
pairs.negative: The playground was full of children laughing and I loved the sound of it.

pairs.positive: Cooking for one feels pointless when you used to cook for two.
pairs.negative: Cooking for the family fills the kitchen with chatter and good smells.

pairs.positive: The winter felt longer and colder this year without anyone to share it with.
pairs.negative: The winter felt cozy and short with someone to share hot cocoa with.

pairs.positive: I forced a smile at the party but inside I was falling apart.
pairs.negative: I couldn't stop grinning at the party because I was having such a great time.

pairs.positive: The letter I never sent sits in my drawer, full of words left unsaid.
pairs.negative: The letter I sent brought a reply that made my heart swell with warmth.

pairs.positive: Walking through the old neighborhood only deepened the ache of nostalgia.
pairs.negative: Walking through the old neighborhood brought back cheerful memories of growing up.

pairs.positive: The movie's ending broke me and I cried quietly in the dark theater.
pairs.negative: The movie's ending was uplifting and I walked out of the theater beaming.

pairs.positive: I lost interest in hobbies that used to bring me so much pleasure.
pairs.negative: I rediscovered a hobby that reignited a spark of excitement in me.

pairs.positive: The dog keeps waiting by the door for someone who won't come back.
pairs.negative: The dog runs to the door every evening when its owner comes home.

pairs.positive: I couldn't bring myself to eat, the food just tasted like nothing.
pairs.negative: The meal was delicious and I savored every single bite.

pairs.positive: The birthday passed without a single call, and I pretended not to care.
pairs.negative: The birthday was full of calls and messages from people who remembered.

pairs.positive: Watching couples in the park only reminded me of what I'd lost.
pairs.negative: Watching couples in the park reminded me how lucky I am to have someone.

pairs.positive: The silence in the house after everyone left was deafening.
pairs.negative: The house buzzed with energy and conversation long after everyone arrived.

pairs.positive: I drove past our old meeting spot and had to pull over to collect myself.
pairs.negative: I drove past our favorite spot and felt a rush of happy memories.

pairs.positive: The journal entries from last year read like dispatches from a different, broken person.
pairs.negative: The journal entries from last year read like notes from someone full of life.

pairs.positive: Even the sunlight felt harsh and unwelcome on days like this.
pairs.negative: The sunlight felt like a warm embrace on a day like this.

pairs.positive: I packed away their belongings but couldn't bring myself to close the box.
pairs.negative: I unpacked the new belongings with excitement, finding a place for everything.

pairs.positive: The ocean looked vast and indifferent, just like the world without them.
pairs.negative: The ocean looked vast and beautiful, full of possibility and wonder.

pairs.positive: I kept checking my phone, hoping for a message that would never come.
pairs.negative: My phone buzzed with a message that instantly made me smile.

pairs.positive: The tree we planted together is the only thing that still grows.
pairs.negative: The tree we planted together is thriving, just like our friendship.

pairs.positive: Nothing feels permanent anymore, and that terrifies me.
pairs.negative: Everything feels stable and secure, and that brings me such comfort.
